sentence_reviews.text: External-data tools connect a model or agent to databases, APIs, files, search engines, sensors, and continuously changing operational state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports agents using percepts/actions and external information; the cited sources broadly support tool/data access concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: The exact list of databases, APIs, files, search engines, sensors, and live state is broader than the citation.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of External databases, APIs & live tools is to connect Caches, APIs & tool interfaces, Databases & information retrieval to downstream capabilities including Tool use & function calling, ReAct-style reasoning and acting, API gateways, tool registries & secure connectors.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support several underlying concepts, but the downstream capability list is atlas-derived rather than directly evidenced.
sentence_reviews.notes: Treat as a graph-positioning claim; dependencies are plausible but the full downstream list needs direct source support.

sentence_reviews.text: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Caches, APIs & tool interfaces, Databases & information retrieval.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The stale/private/contradictory/incorrectly ranked limitation is generally plausible for memory and retrieval systems, but most packets cite only broad background sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: The limitation should be narrowed or backed by a memory/retrieval safety source; dependency list portion is graph-derived.

sentence_reviews.text: External databases, APIs & live tools uses Caches, APIs & tool interfaces as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The packet references support cache_tool_interfaces or the node mechanism broadly, but the dependency rationale is graph-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is plausible but should be verified with a dependency-specific source for exact wording.
